text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Okonkwo, Renata
MRN: [REDACTED]
SPECIMEN ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
CLINICAL HISTORY: 39-year-old premenopausal female, BRCA1 germline pathogenic variant carrier, with previously diagnosed left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 3, triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-), cT2 N1 M0 (Stage IIB). Patient completed neoadjuvant systemic therapy per KEYNOTE-522 protocol: dose-dense doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide x4 cycles followed by weekly paclitaxel x12 weeks, both combined with pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks. Pre-surgical restaging MRI demonstrated residual 1.4 x 1.1 cm mass in the left upper outer quadrant, with mildly abnormal left axillary lymph node (0.9 cm). Elected bilateral nipple-sparing mastectomy given BRCA1 carrier status.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMENS SUBMITTED
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
A: Left breast, nipple-sparing mastectomy specimen (labeled "left breast")
B: Left sentinel lymph node (labeled "left sentinel node, hot and blue")
C: Left axillary contents, lymph node dissection (labeled "left axillary dissection, levels I and II")
D: Right breast, prophylactic nipple-sparing mastectomy specimen (labeled "right breast")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A - LEFT BREAST, NIPPLE-SPARING MASTECTOMY:
Received fresh and subsequently inked and serially sectioned. The specimen consists of a nipple-sparing mastectomy specimen weighing 420 grams and measuring 17.0 x 14.0 x 4.5 cm. The overlying skin ellipse is absent (nipple-sparing approach). The specimen is inked as follows: anterior margin blue, posterior margin black, superior margin green, inferior margin orange. On serial sectioning, in the upper outer quadrant, at approximately 10 o'clock and 4 cm from the nipple bed, there is a firm, gray-white, stellate area measuring 1.3 x 0.9 x 0.8 cm. This area has an ill-defined, irregular border with surrounding fibrotic stroma. The cut surface demonstrates pale gray-white to yellowish discoloration with focal chalky-white deposits, consistent with treatment-related calcification and necrosis. The surrounding breast parenchyma is otherwise unremarkable with predominantly fibrous and fibrofatty tissue. No additional discrete masses are identified. The retroareolar tissue and nipple base are grossly unremarkable. The posterior (deep) margin over the area of interest is estimated at 0.9 cm. Multiple sections submitted including the area of interest in its entirety (sections A1-A12, with A1-A2 representing nipple bed sections, A3-A10 representing the area of interest with margins, and A11-A12 representing uninvolved breast parenchyma).

SPECIMEN B - LEFT SENTINEL LYMPH NODE:
Received fresh, labeled with radioisotope counts ex vivo (hot). A single lymph node is identified measuring 1.2 x 0.8 x 0.6 cm. The node is bivalved and the cut surface is gray-tan and homogeneous without grossly evident tumor. The node is entirely submitted in one cassette (B1) for frozen section evaluation and permanent sections with cytokeratin immunohistochemistry.

SPECIMEN C - LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE DISSECTION, LEVELS I AND II:
Received fresh and fixed in formalin. The specimen consists of fibrofatty tissue measuring 9.5 x 7.0 x 3.0 cm and weighing 48 grams. The specimen is carefully examined and dissected; a total of 14 lymph nodes are identified ranging from 0.3 cm to 1.8 cm in greatest dimension. The two largest nodes (1.8 cm and 1.4 cm) demonstrate firm gray-white cortical areas on cut section. All nodes are entirely submitted. Sections C1-C14 (one node per cassette for nodes >0.5 cm; smaller nodes combined).

SPECIMEN D - RIGHT BREAST, PROPHYLACTIC NIPPLE-SPARING MASTECTOMY:
Received fresh and subsequently fixed. The specimen weighs 385 grams and measures 16.0 x 13.5 x 4.0 cm. The overlying skin is absent (nipple-sparing approach). The specimen is inked and serially sectioned. The cut surface shows predominantly fibrofatty and fibroglandular tissue without discrete masses, cysts, or areas of induration. The retroareolar tissue and nipple base are unremarkable. Representative sections submitted in cassettes D1-D8, including nipple base and representative sections from each quadrant.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTRAOPERATIVE CONSULTATION (FROZEN SECTION)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Frozen section performed on Specimen B (left sentinel lymph node):
Intraoperative impression: No definitive carcinoma identified on frozen section. Note: Isolated tumor cells cannot be excluded; permanent sections with immunohistochemistry to follow.
Communicated to the operating surgeon intraoperatively.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A - LEFT BREAST, NIPPLE-SPARING MASTECTOMY:
Histologic sections through the area of gross induration in the upper outer quadrant reveal residual invasive ductal carcinoma, no special type (NST), histologic grade 3 (Nottingham grading system: tubule formation score 3, nuclear pleomorphism score 3, mitotic count score 2; total 8/9). The residual viable tumor is present as scattered nests and cords of cells distributed within extensive areas of treatment-associated fibrosis. Tumor cells retain the morphologic features of the original diagnostic biopsy: large cells with prominent nucleoli, open chromatin, and moderate to abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are reduced compared to the pre-treatment biopsy (reflecting treatment effect). Approximately 60-70% of the tumor bed area demonstrates treatment effect, characterized by: (1) extensive hyalinized fibrosis with hemosiderin-laden macrophages; (2) foamy macrophage infiltrates and cholesterol cleft formation; (3) areas of coagulative necrosis with dystrophic calcification; (4) scattered lymphocytic infiltrate, including residual tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs estimated at 20-25% of the residual stroma — decreased from pre-treatment levels). The residual viable tumor area involves approximately 30-40% of the total tumor bed, occupying a zone measuring approximately 1.3 x 0.9 x 0.8 cm. No lymphovascular invasion is identified in the post-treatment specimen on H&E-stained sections or on immunohistochemical staining with CD31 and D2-40. No perineural invasion identified. The background breast parenchyma shows no residual DCIS (ductal carcinoma in situ). No additional foci of carcinoma are identified outside the area of interest. The retroareolar ducts and nipple base are free of carcinoma on multiple sections. Surgical margins: The posterior (deep) margin is the closest, with viable tumor nests present 8 mm from the inked margin. All other margins (anterior, superior, inferior, medial, lateral) are greater than 10 mm clear. All surgical margins are negative for carcinoma, invasive or in situ.

SPECIMEN B - LEFT SENTINEL LYMPH NODE:
Permanent sections with H&E staining and immunohistochemistry (cytokeratin AE1/AE3 cocktail): A single focus of isolated tumor cells (ITC) is identified within the subcapsular sinus, consisting of a cluster of 5-6 cytokeratin-positive epithelial cells measuring 0.15 mm in greatest dimension. The cluster does not meet criteria for a micrometastasis (>0.2 mm) or macrometastasis (>2.0 mm). No extranodal extension. Classification: ypN0(i+). Note: The background lymph node architecture shows scattered lymphocytic infiltrates and sinus histiocytosis.

SPECIMEN C - LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE DISSECTION, LEVELS I AND II:
Fourteen lymph nodes are examined in total (14/14 nodes evaluated). Two of 14 lymph nodes (2/14) contain metastatic carcinoma consistent with infiltrating ductal carcinoma, showing morphologic features concordant with the primary tumor. The deposits show treatment effect (fibrosis, necrosis) in addition to viable carcinoma cells. The larger of the two positive nodes (Node #3, 1.8 cm) contains a metastatic deposit measuring 4 mm in greatest dimension. The second positive node (Node #7, 1.4 cm) contains a metastatic deposit measuring 2 mm in greatest dimension. Both deposits are consistent with viable post-treatment metastatic carcinoma. No extranodal extension is identified in either node. The remaining 12 lymph nodes are negative for metastatic carcinoma (12/14 negative). No treatment-related changes (fibrosis, necrosis without viable tumor) are noted in several negative nodes, suggesting prior lymph node involvement with treatment response. Classification: ypN1 (2/14 nodes positive, largest deposit 4 mm, no extranodal extension).

SPECIMEN D - RIGHT BREAST, PROPHYLACTIC NIPPLE-SPARING MASTECTOMY:
Histologic sections from all quadrants of the right breast and the retroareolar/nipple base region are examined. The breast parenchyma consists of fibrous and fibrofatty tissue with lobules showing varying degrees of involution. No invasive carcinoma identified. No ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) identified. No atypical ductal hyperplasia (ADH) or atypical lobular hyperplasia (ALH). No lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS). No evidence of malignancy. Fibrocystic changes including mild adenosis and scattered benign cysts are noted, consistent with age-appropriate changes. Nipple base: No Paget disease. No carcinoma.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on residual tumor in Specimen A):

Estrogen Receptor (ER) — Clone SP1, Ventana BenchMark:
Result: NEGATIVE
Allred Score: 0/8 (0% staining intensity, 0% proportion)
Interpretation: ER negative. No change in receptor status from diagnostic biopsy.

Progesterone Receptor (PR) — Clone 1E2, Ventana BenchMark:
Result: NEGATIVE
Allred Score: 0/8 (0% staining, 0% proportion)
Interpretation: PR negative. No change in receptor status from diagnostic biopsy.

HER2/neu (IHC) — Clone 4B5, Ventana BenchMark ULTRA:
Result: 1+ (NEGATIVE)
Staining pattern: Incomplete membrane staining in <10% of tumor cells.
Interpretation: HER2 negative by IHC (1+). Per ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines, reflex FISH is not indicated for IHC 1+ result. No change in receptor status from diagnostic biopsy.

Cytokeratin AE1/AE3 (Specimen B, sentinel lymph node):
Result: Positive — rare individual and small cluster of cytokeratin-positive cells identified in subcapsular sinus (see microscopic description). Consistent with isolated tumor cells (ITC), 0.15 mm cluster.

CD31 (endothelial marker, Specimen A):
Result: No evidence of lymphovascular invasion. Tumor nests confirmed to be within fibrotic stroma, not within vascular spaces.

D2-40 (lymphatic marker, Specimen A):
Result: No evidence of lymphovascular invasion identified.

Ki-67 PROLIFERATION INDEX (Specimen A, residual tumor):
Result: Approximately 35% (decreased from pre-treatment value of 85%, consistent with treatment effect on proliferating cell population).

PENDING STUDIES:
- Residual Cancer Burden (RCB) calculation: Formal RCB index calculation pending completion of measurements and standardized worksheet. Preliminary RCB class determination: RCB Class II (intermediate residual disease) based on residual tumor dimensions (1.3 x 0.9 cm in primary, 2/14 positive axillary nodes with largest deposit 4 mm). Formal RCB score to be provided in addendum.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. LEFT BREAST, NIPPLE-SPARING MASTECTOMY:
   1. RESIDUAL INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA, NO SPECIAL TYPE (NST), GRADE 3
      - Tumor size (residual viable tumor): 1.3 x 0.9 x 0.8 cm (ypT1b)
      - Tumor bed dimensions (including treatment effect zone): approximately 1.6 x 1.2 x 1.0 cm
      - Nottingham grade: 3 (score 8/9; tubule formation 3, nuclear pleomorphism 3, mitotic count 2)
      - Treatment effect: Present, extensive (approximately 60-70% of tumor bed replaced by necrosis, fibrosis, and foamy macrophage infiltrates)
      - Lymphovascular invasion: NOT IDENTIFIED
      - Perineural invasion: NOT IDENTIFIED
      - DCIS component: Not identified
      - Margins: ALL NEGATIVE (closest margin: posterior/deep, 8 mm)
      - Nipple involvement: NOT IDENTIFIED
      - Skin involvement: NOT IDENTIFIED
      - ER: Negative (0%), PR: Negative (0%), HER2: 1+ (Negative)
      - Ki-67 on residual tumor: approximately 35%

B. LEFT SENTINEL LYMPH NODE (1 node examined):
   - Isolated tumor cells (ITC) detected: 0.15 mm cluster, cytokeratin AE1/AE3 positive
   - Classification: ypN0(i+)
   - Extranodal extension: NOT IDENTIFIED

C. LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE DISSECTION, LEVELS I AND II (14 nodes examined):
   - Metastatic carcinoma in 2 of 14 lymph nodes (2/14)
   - Largest metastatic deposit: 4 mm
   - Second deposit: 2 mm
   - Extranodal extension: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - Classification: ypN1 (macrometastases in 2 nodes, no ENE)

D. RIGHT BREAST, PROPHYLACTIC NIPPLE-SPARING MASTECTOMY:
   - NO CARCINOMA IDENTIFIED
   - No ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS)
   - No lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS)
   - No atypical hyperplasia
   - Fibrocystic changes, benign

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, post-neoadjuvant, ypTNM)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ypT: ypT1b (residual invasive carcinoma, 1.3 cm greatest dimension)
ypN: ypN1 (metastasis in 2 of 14 regional lymph nodes, largest deposit 4 mm; sentinel node with ITC classified as ypN0(i+) per AJCC 8th edition is superseded by ypN1 from axillary dissection)
ypM: Not applicable (no distant metastasis clinically)
OVERALL PATHOLOGIC STAGE: ypT1b ypN1 M0
RESIDUAL CANCER BURDEN: RCB Class II (intermediate) — formal RCB score addendum pending
PATHOLOGIC COMPLETE RESPONSE (pCR): NOT ACHIEVED (non-pCR)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
This post-neoadjuvant surgical specimen demonstrates a non-pathologic complete response (non-pCR) to neoadjuvant chemotherapy and immunotherapy. The residual tumor shows morphologic evidence of treatment effect including extensive fibrosis, necrosis, and macrophage infiltrates, with a reduction in Ki-67 proliferation index from 85% (pre-treatment) to approximately 35% (post-treatment). Receptor status (ER/PR/HER2) is unchanged from the diagnostic core needle biopsy, confirming the triple-negative phenotype is retained in residual disease. The presence of 2 of 14 positive lymph nodes with viable tumor deposits (ypN1) combined with residual primary tumor (ypT1b) and the absence of pathologic complete response has prognostic implications. The right breast (prophylactic mastectomy) shows no evidence of malignancy, consistent with the clinical context of BRCA1-associated risk-reducing surgery.
